description:
**Milestone:** Phase 2: Component Expansion

Build hx-dialog: modal/non-modal dialog with focus trap, backdrop, animations. Required for confirmations, forms, media previews. Uses native <dialog> element internally. Trigger via Drupal behavior; composed: true events cross Shadow DOM.

**Files to Modify:**
- packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-dialog/

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Modal and non-modal modes
- [ ] Focus trap (tab cycling within dialog)
- [ ] Click-outside-to-close (configurable)
- [ ] Escape key closes
- [ ] CSS parts: dialog, backdrop, header, body, footer
- [ ] Slots: default, header, footer
- [ ] Events: hx-open, hx-close, hx-cancel
- [ ] ARIA: role=dialog, aria-modal, aria-labelledby
- [ ] <5KB gzipped

**Complexity:** large
title: Implement hx-dialog component

<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-dialog/hx-dialog.stories.ts`:
- Line 460: Remove the non-null assertions from the Storybook play functions
(e.g., the usage of triggerBtn!.click()) and replace them with explicit guard or
fail-fast checks: locate the play functions in hx-dialog.stories.ts that
reference DOM elements (such as triggerBtn) and change the logic to first query
the element, then either return/throw a clear error if the element is
null/undefined or proceed after confirming existence; apply the same pattern to
the other affected ranges (around lines 547-557 and 566) so no `!` non-null
assertions remain and any missing element is handled explicitly.
- Around line 61-67: Two stories incorrectly use the literal attribute
close-on-backdrop="false" which Lit treats as truthy; update those instances
(the story usages referenced around the render and the other occurrence) to set
the property instead using property binding: replace close-on-backdrop="false"
with .closeOnBackdrop=${false} on the <hx-dialog> element (leave the existing
correct ?close-on-backdrop binding in the default render alone). Ensure both
occurrences (the one near the render and the other around the second occurrence)
use .closeOnBackdrop=${false} so the dialog property is actually false.

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-dialog/hx-dialog.styles.ts`:
- Around line 26-130: Several CSS rules (notably .dialog, dialog::backdrop,
.dialog-backdrop, .dialog__header, .dialog__heading, .dialog__body,
.dialog__footer) contain literal fallback values (hex, rem, ms, etc.); replace
those hardcoded literals with token-only fallbacks so every var(...) uses only
--hx-* tokens (e.g., instead of var(--hx-dialog-bg, `#ffffff`) use
var(--hx-dialog-bg, var(--hx-color-neutral-0)

threadFeedback.agentReasoning: Valid bug. In Lit 3, `close-on-backdrop="false"` sets the boolean property to `true` because attribute presence (regardless of value) maps to true with the default Boolean converter. Stories 4 and 10 are broken — backdrop won't actually be disabled. Fix is required for correctness.

Project CLAUDE.md explicitly forbids non-null assertions. The `triggerBtn!.click()`, `dialog!.addEventListener(...)`, `openBtn!.click()`, and `closeBtn!.click()` in stories violate this. Guard-based checks are correct replacements.

CLAUDE.md forbids hardcoded values. The styles have third-level literal fallbacks (`#ffffff`, `0.5rem`, `200ms`, etc.) embedded inside token chains. These should be removed — the token chain `var(--hx-dialog-bg, var(--hx-color-neutral-0))` is sufficient without a raw hex terminal.

Nitpick/trivial. The 50ms setTimeout is a pragmatic workaround for slot assignment timing in Vitest browser mode — slotchange events in shadow DOM have known async ordering issues. Replacing with slotchange listeners risks introducing new flakiness (slotchange may fire before assignedNodes are populated). Tests pass and this is a test-infrastructure concern, not a correctness issue.

Valid gap — the test suite only covers `hx-close` via the cancel/Escape path. The JSDoc documents `hx-close` fires "when the dialog closes for any reason." Once thread PRRT_l0's fix adds `hx-close` emission to `_closeDialog()`, there must be a test for it. CLAUDE.md: "no untested code, every public API has tests."

CLAUDE.md: "No non-null assertions." The `_dialogEl!` assertion violates this. The existing runtime null guards (`if (!dialog) return`) throughout the component already handle the null case correctly. Changing to `HTMLDialogElement | null` type makes the TypeScript strict enforcement explicit.

Real bug confirmed by CodeRabbit's web query of Lit 3 docs. `close-on-backdrop="false"` sets `closeOnBackdrop = true` via Lit's default Boolean converter. The `NoBackdropClose` story, `DangerConfirmation` story, and consumer HTML usage are all broken for the "false" case. A custom converter is required for Drupal/HTML compatibility.

CRITICAL: Real contract violation. JSDoc states `hx-close` fires "when the dialog closes for any reason." The `_closeDialog()` path (called by `close()` and `open = false`) emits nothing. Only `_cancel()` emits `hx-close`. Consumers relying on `hx-close` for state management (as documented) will miss normal close events. The EventFiring story even tests close-button behavior expecting the event. This must be fixed.

CRITICAL: Real rendering bug. `_renderHeader()` checks `!hasHeading && !this._hasHeaderSlot` and returns `nothing` on first render when only header slot content is provided (no `heading` attribute). Since the slot never renders, `slotchange` never fires, `_hasHeaderSlot` stays false forever. The `WithCustomHeader` story silently broken. Fix: check light DOM for `[slot="header"]` before bailing.

Correct. The `test-results/.last-run.json` is an ephemeral Playwright artifact with `"status": "failed"` that should not be tracked in version control. It creates confusing signals in repo history and CI.
